title: filix-foemina aquilina var. pseudocaudata
intro: Filix-foemina aquilina var. pseudocaudata, also known as the Lady fern, is a type of fern plant that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. Traditionally, the Lady fern has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been employed to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its health benefits, including its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the Lady fern is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
